title: Inside DoorDash's Cautious Expansion Into Ghost Kitchens
text: DoorDash has opened its second ghost kitchen and introduced a new model where DoorDash handles most operations, from hiring to cooking to supply chain and logistics. The new DoorDash Kitchens location opened in a mall in San Jose, California, and currently houses six restaurants including Canter's Deli, Milk Bar and Curry Up Now, which have licensed their brands to DoorDash and worked with the company to optimize menus. The company takes on the full cost of operations and shares revenue with the restaurants. This represents a shift from the first DoorDash Kitchens opened in 2019 in Redwood City, where restaurant tenants rented space and used their own employees. DoorDash is expanding ghost kitchen presence by leasing kitchens on behalf of top-performing brands. This pickup and delivery-only service model lets restaurants test new markets while avoiding the time and expense of opening storefronts. The strategy helps restaurant partners scale efficiently while allowing DoorDash to deepen its involvement in food production and operations.
